content: All facility parameters should be traceable to specific document page references

Executive summary must include a bottom_line section with 3-5 sentences containing specific diligence items for the IC. Generic conclusions like "portfolio looks healthy" are insufficient. Each diligence item must reference specific data points (e.g., "PAR30 at 3.6% is within the 5% covenant threshold but trending upward from 2.8% in Q3").

Collection Rate = GLR (Gross Liquidation Rate): all cash collected divided by face value of originated deals. This is the headline metric. CRR (Capital Recovery Rate) = collected divided by purchase price (what the fund actually paid). CRR is shown separately in the Returns tab, not mixed with GLR. See ANALYSIS_FRAMEWORK.md Section 8.

PAR without contractual benchmarks: hide the metric, do not estimate. Estimated DPD from shortfall proxies can mislead IC audiences who interpret PAR as a hard contractual delinquency measure. Exception: Option C uses empirical collection timing curves from 50+ completed deals to infer expected collection milestones. When Option C is used, it must carry a "Derived from historical patterns" badge.

Backdated views (as_of_date < snapshot_date) must block all AI outputs. Balance columns (Collected, Denied, Outstanding, Pending) reflect the tape snapshot date, not the as-of date. AI commentary would present inflated collection rates and understated risk as fact. Safe metrics on backdated views: deal count, originated volume, deployment, vintage composition. Unsafe: everything using balance columns.

Every AI finding must cite specific numbers from the data. Findings like "collection rates are declining" are rejected. Required format: "Collection rate declined from 91.2% (Q3 2025) to 87.8% (Q1 2026), driven by Vintage 2025-H2 underperformance at 79.3% vs portfolio average of 89.1%." Data gaps must be flagged with a DATA GAPS section rather than silently omitted.
